_description: Difficulty coordination map. Maps day ranges to target difficulty levels for all sections.
_usage: generate.sh reads this to select appropriately-leveled topics. Each section tracks its own index, but this map signals the intended difficulty window.

phases.name: Foundation
phases.description: Build confidence. All sections at easy/intro level.

system_design.focus: Fundamentals — HTTP, DNS, load balancing, basic databases

ai.focus: What is ML, supervised vs unsupervised, basic concepts

phases.name: Growth
phases.description: Mix of easy and medium. Introduce complexity gradually.

system_design.focus: Caching, message queues, database design, rate limiting

algorithms.focus: Sliding Window, Binary Search, Linked Lists, Trees

ai.focus: Neural nets, embeddings, LLMs, prompt engineering

phases.name: Mastery
phases.description: Medium difficulty across all sections. Solid competency.

system_design.focus: Distributed systems, consensus, microservices, event sourcing

soft_skills.focus: Staff-level scenarios — org impact, cross-team, vision

frontend.focus: Next.js, SSR, web workers, advanced patterns

ai.focus: Fine-tuning, RAG, agents, AI system design

system_design.focus: Large-scale real systems — YouTube, Twitter, Uber architecture

algorithms.focus: Hard DP, advanced graphs, bit manipulation, math

soft_skills.focus: Org design, technical strategy, executive communication

frontend.focus: Browser internals, rendering pipelines, custom frameworks

ai.focus: AI safety, cutting-edge research, production ML systems

_note: Days 51+ are always 'Expert' phase. Use phases[3] for those.
